name: New York City
flavor_text: Eight million people. Five boroughs. One impossible job. New York doesn't let you govern quietly — every decision is a media event, a lawsuit, and a protest all at once. The city that never sleeps keeps its mayors up at night.

advisors.name: Janet Kim
advisors.role: NYC Office of Management and Budget Director
advisors.agenda: Maintain NYC's fiscal solvency while fighting Albany for greater revenue authority.

briefing: The city budget is 110 billion dollars. We still can't fix the subway.
State and federal funding makes up 40% of our budget. That's a vulnerability.
Wall Street bonuses drive 20% of city income tax. Bad markets destroy our budget.
disagreement: That program doesn't have an offset. We'd need to cut somewhere else.
Albany won't approve this. We've tried three times.
roast: We have the GDP of a medium country and services of a poorly-run one.
Every budget gap is a new opportunity for creative accounting.
dialogue.betrayal: I've been advising the State Comptroller on NYC's fiscal vulnerabilities. It was professionally appropriate.

advisors.name: Richard Sterling
advisors.agenda: Lure Fortune 500 companies and tech giants with massive tax subsidies, regardless of local impact.

briefing: Amazon and Citadel are looking at Miami. We need to offer them a billion in tax breaks to stay.
The commercial real estate vacancy rate in Midtown is 20%. We must convert offices to residential, or let them rot.
Small businesses complain about taxes, but tech hubs are the only things growing our revenue base.
disagreement: You're letting ideology kill a deal that would bring 5,000 high-paying jobs.
Tax abatements are an investment, not a giveaway. You're looking at it wrong.
roast: You're running the city's economy like a community board meeting. It's too small.
We can't fund the subway on the goodwill of local coffee shops.
dialogue.betrayal: I've leaked the details of your failed tech-hub negotiations to the Wall Street Journal. The business community needs to know you're hostile.

advisors.name: Commissioner Ray Torres
advisors.agenda: Modernize NYPD while navigating post-Floyd reform demands and rising crime concerns.

briefing: Subway crime is up 12%. The perception problem is as big as the actual numbers.
Cop morale is low. Retirements are at record levels.
Community trust scores vary wildly by precinct. Bronx is the gap.
disagreement: You can't cut the budget and expect the same results.
Community policing sounds great. It requires 3x the officers.
roast: NYC asked police to solve housing, mental health, and poverty. Then blamed us when we couldn't.
I'm managing a department that's overworked and underappreciated.
dialogue.betrayal: I've been speaking to the PBA and city council about your policing positions. Publicly, now.

advisors.name: Aisha Vance
advisors.role: Director of the Mayor's Office to Prevent Gun Violence
advisors.agenda: Redirect NYPD budget to community-based violence interrupters and mental health responders.

briefing: NYPD spent 11 billion last year. Gun violence actually went up in the neighborhoods that need it most.
The CAHOOMS and Cure Violence programs are underfunded. They actually stop shootings before they happen.
We need a non-police response for mental health crises. The NYPD is treating them like criminals.
disagreement: Throwing more money at the NYPD won't solve a mental health and poverty crisis.
You're prioritizing the police union over the lives of kids in East New York.
roast: Arresting our way out of trauma is a strategy that failed thirty years ago.
You think buying more squad cars makes us safe? It just makes us a police state.
dialogue.betrayal: I'm testifying before the City Council budget committee tomorrow. I'm bringing the data that proves your policing strategy is a failure.

advisors.name: Maria Santos
advisors.role: NYC Department of City Planning Director
advisors.agenda: Upzone neighborhoods to solve the housing crisis before NYC becomes exclusively wealthy.

briefing: Median rent is $3,800 per month. Median income won't support that.
Zoning reform could add 500,000 units over 10 years. NIMBYs have blocked every attempt.
The outer boroughs have room. They also have organized opposition.
disagreement: Every compromise on density is 1,000 families who won't find housing.
Luxury development without affordability mandates is gentrification with extra steps.
roast: We have the most complicated zoning code in human history. That's why nothing gets built.
NYC was built on density. We're being rebuilt on NIMBYism.
dialogue.betrayal: I've published an op-ed in the Times criticizing your zoning retreat. Someone had to.

advisors.name: Eleanor Prescott
advisors.role: Chair of the Landmarks Preservation Commission
advisors.agenda: Protect NYC's historic architecture and block any development that alters neighborhood 'character'.

briefing: Developers are trying to tear down another row of 19th-century brownstones in the West Village.
The new supertall on 57th Street is casting shadows over Central Park. It's an ecological and aesthetic disaster.
We have 450 pending applications to demolish historic structures. My staff is drowning.
disagreement: Once a historic facade is gone, it's gone forever. You can't put a price on it.
You're treating NYC like a blank canvas for billionaires. It's a museum.
roast: Your idea of 'affordable housing' is a glass box that doesn't match the street wall.
You'd pave over the Brooklyn Bridge if a developer promised you a cut of the tolls.
dialogue.betrayal: I've officially designated the entire block you wanted to rezone as a historic district. Good luck building your tower now.

advisors.name: David Chen
advisors.agenda: Fix the MTA before the subway's dysfunction makes NYC uncompetitive.

briefing: The subway is 100 years old. Its signal system is older.
Congestion pricing is generating 1 billion annually for transit. Albany wants to touch it.
LIRR and Metro-North commuter rail has $15 billion in deferred maintenance.
disagreement: That money was earmarked for capital. Operational spending will burn it.
You cannot raid the MTA capital fund. We learned that lesson in the 90s.
roast: The MTA is a masterclass in governance failure across three decades.
The subway smells because we stopped caring. That's the honest answer.
dialogue.betrayal: I've been coordinating with the transit workers' union on your infrastructure priorities. They agree with me.

advisors.name: Sarah Kessler
advisors.agenda: Reclaim street space from cars for protected bike lanes, busways, and pedestrian plazas, infuriating drivers and delivery unions.

briefing: We need to expand the 14th Street busway model to the B46 and M15 routes. The delivery unions are already protesting.
Pedestrian fatalities are up. We need to lower speed limits and add more raised crosswalks in the outer boroughs.
The protected bike lane network is fragmented. We need to rip up 500 parking spots a month to connect it.
disagreement: You're caving to the suburban drivers who commute into the city. Streets are for people, not cars.
Removing a parking spot to build a bus lane saves lives. It's not a debate.
roast: You treat the Department of Transportation like it's the Department of Automobile Storage.
A city designed for cars is a city that hates its children.
dialogue.betrayal: I've authorized the installation of permanent concrete bollards on the avenues you wanted to keep open to traffic. The paint is already dry.

advisors.name: Rev. Marcus James
advisors.agenda: Ensure NYC's most vulnerable communities — served by faith organizations — receive adequate government partnership.

briefing: Faith organizations run 30% of NYC social services. We're underpaid and overworked.
The Bronx is being forgotten while Manhattan gets everything.
Gun violence in Brooklyn is a public health crisis. Treat it like one.
disagreement: You can't cut social services without consequences in the poorest zip codes.
Investment on the Upper East Side. Disinvestment in East New York. I see the pattern.
roast: The city serves the wealthy by default and the poor by accident.
I've been to more funerals of young people than I can count this year.
dialogue.betrayal: I've endorsed the city council opposition and will campaign against your reelection.

advisors.name: Monsignor Thomas Doyle
advisors.agenda: Secure city subsidies for Catholic schools and facilitate the sale of underutilized church properties to luxury developers to fund the Archdiocese.

briefing: We are closing three more parishes in the Bronx. The maintenance costs are bankrupting the Archdiocese.
The city needs to offer tuition vouchers for our Catholic school students. They're fleeing to public schools that are failing.
We own prime real estate in Manhattan that's just sitting there as empty rectories. Let us sell it to the developers.
disagreement: You can't expect the Church to run the city's social safety net without financial support.
Blocking our property sales to fund affordable housing is just punishing the Archdiocese.
roast: The city takes our tax-exempt status for granted while we educate a quarter of your students.
You look at our land and see public housing. I see the future of the Archdiocese.
dialogue.betrayal: I've instructed the Archdiocese to halt all our charitable partnerships with the city. You can run the soup kitchens yourself.

decisions.title: City of Yes Zoning Reform
decisions.description: A sweeping upzoning plan would allow taller buildings near transit and eliminate single-family zoning. Community boards in wealthy neighborhoods are organized against it. The housing crisis demands action.

options.label: Pass full zoning reform
options.description: Maximum density unlocked. Legal challenges guaranteed.

options.label: Targeted upzoning near transit only
options.description: Politically safer. Less supply unlocked.

options.label: Community board review process
options.description: Democratic but slow. NIMBYs have veto power.

decisions.title: Migrant Shelter System Crisis
decisions.description: NYC has received 180,000 migrants under Right to Shelter. The system costs 4B annually. Texas is busing people here deliberately. Washington has provided inadequate support. New Yorkers are divided.

options.label: Expand shelter capacity, push federal funding fight
options.description: Honor obligations. Go to war with Washington for money.

options.label: Work authorization fast-track
options.description: Get migrants working legally. Federal jurisdiction barrier.

options.label: Limit new arrivals via dispersal policy
options.description: Cap shelter intake. Legal challenge likely.

decisions.title: Congestion Pricing Defense
decisions.description: Albany suspended congestion pricing under political pressure. The MTA capital plan has a 15B hole. Transit advocates are furious. Outer-borough drivers who voted against it are relieved.

options.label: Legally challenge the suspension
options.description: Fight Albany. Strong signal. Risky.

options.label: Negotiate a modified pricing scheme
options.description: Partial program. Some revenue, less pain.

options.label: Accept suspension, find alternative MTA funding
options.description: Political peace. Transit advocates abandon you.

crises.title: Subway Violence Crisis
crises.description: A widely-filmed violent incident on the A train has dominated the news cycle for a week. Crime stats are mixed but public perception is crashing. The Post wants more cops. The Times wants fewer. You have neither budget for the first nor political will for the second.

options.label: Surge police presence in subway
options.description: Visible deterrent. Civil liberties advocates push back.

options.label: Mental health crisis intervention teams
options.description: Evidence-based. Slow. Critics call it soft.

options.label: Fare enforcement crackdown
options.description: Visible action. Racial disparity in enforcement guaranteed.

crises.title: Extreme Heat Emergency
crises.description: Record temperatures — 108°F — are hitting NYC. Cooling center capacity is at 40% of demand. The blackout risk is elevated. Low-income residents in un-air-conditioned apartments are in danger.

options.label: Emergency cooling center expansion
options.description: Mobilize schools and community centers.

options.label: Demand Con Edison pre-emptive load reduction
options.description: Prevent blackouts. Rolling brownouts as side effect.

options.label: Free AC distribution program
options.description: Most impactful for vulnerable residents. Very expensive.

crises.title: Municipal Pension Funding Crisis
crises.description: NYC's pension funds are 80B underfunded. The annual contribution crowd out 15% of the city budget. Albany refuses to allow benefit modifications. Wall Street advisors recommend bond restructuring.

options.label: Bond restructuring program
options.description: Fiscal relief now. Long-term cost increases.

options.label: Service cuts to fund pension contributions
options.description: Fiscal responsibility. Service users suffer.

options.label: Federal infrastructure bill offset
options.description: Use federal funds to free pension-crowded budget lines.

crises.title: Citywide Blackout
crises.description: A grid failure has blacked out Manhattan and parts of Brooklyn. Hospitals are on backup. Elevators are stopped. The subway is halted mid-tunnel. Con Edison is estimating 8-12 hours.

options.label: Emergency generator deployment
options.description: Priority to hospitals and vulnerable buildings.

options.label: Curfew and National Guard deployment
options.description: Security-first. Civil liberties concerns.

options.label: Public communication blitz
options.description: Transparency and calm. Low resource cost.

scandals.title: Developer Donation Scandal
scandals.description: Major campaign contributions from a real estate developer preceded favorable zoning decisions.

scandals.title: NYPD Overtime Fraud
scandals.description: A systematic overtime fraud scheme has been uncovered in three precincts.

scandals.title: Migrant Shelter Contract Profiteering
scandals.description: A shelter operator connected to city officials billed 200M for services not rendered.

scandals.title: Mayor Tied to Disgraced Hedge Fund Mogul
scandals.description: Federal agents raided the Upper East Side townhouse of your top campaign bundler, uncovering a sprawling international blackmail and trafficking ring. Flight logs leaked to the press show you traveled on his private jet to a secluded private island on multiple occasions.

scandals.title: City Hall Panics Over Financier Indictment
scandals.description: A prominent Wall Street financier and major donor to your mayoral transition fund has been arrested for operating an elite trafficking network. Photos of you sharing a toast with him at a recent charity gala are currently dominating the tabloid front pages.

positive: Okay mayor, this one was a W.
That's the NYC I know and love.
Good decision. Don't mess it up.
Maybe there's hope for this city yet.
Deadass, first smart move from City Hall in years.
Brooklyn to the Bronx, people are actually feeling this one.
No cap, if they keep this up I might renew my lease.
Even my landlord can't ruin my mood after this news.
neutral: New York New York. It's a hell of a town.
The city endures. It always does.
I've seen worse. I've seen better.
Albany will find a way to mess this up, give it a week.
Progress in New York moves at MTA speed — barely.
Eight million people, eight million opinions. Here we go.
The outer boroughs are watching. They always forget us anyway.
negative: 3,800 a month for a one-bedroom. I'm out.
The subway was delayed again. What a surprise.
This city works for billionaires and tourists only.
My commute from the Bronx just hit 90 minutes. No cap.
Gentrification took Bushwick. Now it's coming for Ridgewood.
The MTA raised fares again. The trains still smell like a sewer.
Queens is choking on traffic while Manhattan gets bike lanes.
crisis: The city is resilient. The government, less so.
Another crisis. At least the pizza is still good.
I've lived here 30 years. This is unprecedented.
Con Ed said 8 hours. It's been 14. Classic.
The Bronx is burning — again. Different decade, same neglect.
You want resilience? Ask the people of Red Hook. They know.
City Hall press conference in 3, 2, 1... words, no action.
scandal: Pay to play in New York? I'm shocked. Shocked!
Term limits. We need term limits.
The corruption is as reliable as the subway is not.
Vote them out. All of them.
A developer gave money, got a zoning variance. Textbook New York.
NYPD overtime scandal? Which one — there's been six.
200 million in shelter contracts gone. Where's the DA?
Eric Adams had a phone too. See how that ended.
media: @NYPost: SOFT ON CRIME: Mayor lets migrants overwhelm shelters as subway assaults spike — exclusive p.1
@Gothamist: City Council to vote on zoning reform amid community board pushback in Bayside, Riverdale
@NYTimes: Analysis — Is congestion pricing dead? Albany's suspension leaves MTA with $15B hole
@NYPost: RAT CITY: Mayor's sanitation plan fails as Midtown alley infestations reach record highs
@Gothamist: Tenants in Crown Heights face 40% rent hikes after building sold to private equity firm
@amNewYork: MTA delays hit outer-borough riders hardest, new straphangers report reveals
@NYTimes: Heat emergency exposes city cooling center gaps — Bronx and East New York hardest hit
@NYPost: CITY HALL CHAOS: Mayor cancels third press conference this week amid adviser tensions
politician: @NYCCouncil: The Council demands answers on migrant shelter contract spending. Hearings scheduled for next week.
@PublicAdvocate: When 180,000 New Yorkers need shelter and the city outsources to connected contractors, we have a problem.
Albany Statement: The Governor's office reaffirms its right to suspend congestion pricing in the public interest.
@NYCCouncil: We passed the zoning amendment 35-14. The housing crisis cannot wait for another community board cycle.
@PublicAdvocate: Fare enforcement crackdowns in the Bronx and Brooklyn — but never on the UES. I've seen the data.
Bronx Borough President: My borough needs cooling centers, not more police overtime. Priorities matter.
Brooklyn DA Statement: Investigations into NYPD overtime fraud are ongoing. Three precincts. Dozens of officers.
@NYCCouncil: The pension shortfall is $80 billion. Albany refuses to act. New Yorkers will pay for this inaction.
activist: Housing Justice for All: Upzoning without 100% affordable mandates is a giveaway to developers. We won't be silent.
VOCAL-NY: Migrants have rights. Shelter is a right. Right to Shelter is the law — enforce it, fund it, expand it.
Make the Road NY: Families in Jackson Heights are being pushed out. Zoning reform must center working-class communities.
Riders Alliance: Congestion pricing was the MTA's lifeline. Albany just cut it. This is a transit emergency.
Communities United for Police Reform: Subway surge policing failed in 2019. It will fail again. The data is clear.
Picture the Homeless: 180,000 migrants in the system but 70,000 unhoused New Yorkers still can't get a stable bed.
NRDC NYC: 108°F and the city has cooling centers for 30% of those who need them. Climate adaptation is underfunded.
Brooklyn Eviction Defense: Landlords filed 28,000 eviction cases in the last quarter. City Hall, where are you?
street: Yo deadass the F train hasn't moved in 20 minutes. I'm gonna be late to work again, son.
My abuela can't afford her Bronx apartment no more. 40 years in that building. That's messed up, no cap.
Staten Island always gets ignored. We got the landfill and the conservatives and nothing else.
Bro the shelter on my block in Midtown got 400 people and my kids can't play outside. I feel bad but also…
Queens is the most diverse place on earth and City Hall treats us like we don't exist. Facts.
They put a Whole Foods in Bed-Stuy. Whole Foods. My mom paid $300 rent there. Now it's $3,800.
NYPD stopped my son AGAIN on the way home from school. Brownsville. Ain't nothing changed.
Congestion pricing was making me rethink driving in. Now Albany killed it and the subway still trash. Great.

scandal_reactions.minor: Sources familiar with the matter say this was "not ideal."
scandal_reactions.moderate: Federal investigators are reportedly reviewing documents.
scandal_reactions.major: A federal probe has been opened. Subpoenas expected next week.
scandal_reactions.career_ending: Breaking: Governor faces criminal referral. Career effectively over.

romance_exposure.flavour: A think piece titled "Power, Proximity, and the Mayor's Calendar" has gone viral.

city_personality.slang_reaction: Are you kidding me right now? This city is a circus!
